Notice: The link on the homepage directs to forum number f=14, but the Video/Sound forum is f=114. There is a "1" missing. Can someone fix this?

Hi, I have a Dell Inspiron 6000 and this is my xorg.conf:

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.

	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"gb"

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"

EndSection

	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"

	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"

	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"Intel Corporation Mobile 915GM/GMS/910GML Express Graphics Controller"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           8
                Modes           "320x200"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           16
                Modes           "320x200"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "320x200"
        EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"320x240"
	EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           16
                Modes           "320x240"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "320x240"
        EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"640x480"
	EndSubSection

                Depth           16
                Modes           "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "640x480"
        EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"800x600"
	EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           16
                Modes           "800x600"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24

	SubSection "Display"

		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

I would like to know how to use the resolutions I have added (320x200, 320x240, 640x480, 800x600), as I have a game in Wine/Cedega that won't run unless it can set 320x200x8 mode.

---

### Post by jazzmuzik on 2006-04-23
Press Alt-Ctrl-Pluskey or Alt-Ctrl-Minuskey to change resolutions. Note that the plus/minus keys must be the ones on the numeric keypad. The other +/- keys have no effect.

---

### Post by Ubuntuud on 2006-04-23
Aren't you supposed to put the resolution behind each other?
Like: "Modes "600x800" "1280x800" "1024x7...."

---

### Post by jazzmuzik on 2006-04-23
I don't know if it makes a difference or not, but as the previous poster points out you can specify all your resolutions for a particular color depth all on the same line. Here's part of mine:

```
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           1
                Modes           "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           4
                Modes           "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
        EndSubSection

```

The Depth 1 thing specifies a 1 bit depth (2 to the 1 power), or two colors since 1 bit can only store two values, either a 0 or a 1. Depth 4 means 2 to the 4 power or 16 colors and so on up to 24 bit color.

The point is that you could take all your similar bit depths and put them all on the same line. I'm guessing here, but that's what you may have to do to be able to switch resolutions. Wait to hear from others though. I'm just surprised at how your xorg.conf is set up that way.

Another possibility: you can run the xorg reconfigure script and it will automatically figure out all the video modes available on your laptop and recreate the xorg.conf file. Save your old one first though if you try this.

---

### Post by GNULinuxGeek on 2006-11-02
My xorg.conf file does put the resolution values in lateral form.  The issue I am having is that I KNOW my monitor will do 1600x1280, and I have added that to the xorg.conf file.  However, that value will not display when I try to change res.  

I currently running KDE but can switch back to GNOME in a heartbeat.

Any commentary?

GNULinuxGeek

### Post by dj_tcso on 2006-11-05
If you have added that resolution to xorg.conf and you know that your monitor can handle it try to modify

 ~/.kde/share/config/displayconfigrc

You can find current desktop resolution. I've changed it and finally i could run my desktop 1152x864.

---
